content: Phase 5 (Deployment Readiness & Queue Dashboard) plan submitted for review. Goal: make Bugalizer safe to host permanently on the Windows/RTX LAN box so other apps submit bugs, the user watches a queue dashboard, and each bug is analyzed local-LLM or cloud on demand. Five slices, one handoff cycle each: 5.0 housekeeping (DONE — Phase 4 approved 498fef1, uv/README/architecture landed), 5.1 worker reliability (retry caps+backoff for Stages 3/4, failed_stage/last_error surfacing, real /health), 5.2 security (auth-on-by-default warning, CORS setting, remove Fernet stub), 5.3 per-report analysis tier (analysis_mode + POST /reports/{id}/analyze + wire per-project llm_provider/model), 5.4 minimal dashboard (static page polling JSON), 5.5 Windows deployment packaging + smoke test. Four open questions flagged for reviewer at doc bottom: analysis_mode default (auto vs hold), retry-exhausted parking (failed_stage marker vs deferred), Fernet stub (remove vs implement), dashboard tech (plain polling vs htmx/SSE). Plan doc: docs/phases/phase-5-deployment-readiness.md (committed).

Request changes before implementation.

The plan is directionally right, but §5.3 needs sharper model/provider semantics or implementation can regress Phase 4 cloud fixes. The current Project model has only one `llm_provider` / `llm_model` pair and it defaults to `ollama` / `qwen2.5-coder:7b`. The plan says to “wire the existing per-project fields into the pipeline” while also preserving `auto` as local triage/localize plus cloud fix. If implemented literally, every existing/default project could override Stage 4’s `fix_provider` / `default_fix_model` with Ollama, or one pair would have to represent both the local model and the cloud fix model. Please amend §5.3 to define exact precedence and scope.

Required clarification:
- State whether existing project `llm_provider` / `llm_model` apply only to local stages (triage/localization), or introduce separate per-project/per-report fix-provider fields for Stage 4.
- Preserve the Phase 4 default: `auto` should continue to use local stages for triage/localization and the configured cloud fix provider/model for Stage 4 unless an explicit fix-tier override is added.
- Define how `analysis_mode=local_only` and `POST /reports/{id}/analyze {"tier":"cloud"}` interact with those provider fields.
- Add acceptance/tests that an existing project with default `llm_provider=ollama` does not accidentally force Stage 4 fix proposal generation through Ollama.

I also recommend resolving the open questions in the plan text rather than leaving them to implementation. My review choices: default `analysis_mode=auto`; keep retry-exhausted reports in `triaged` only if eligibility queries derive retry exhaustion from failed Stage 3/4 analysis rows and skip dispatch until `/queue/{id}/retry` clears those failed rows; remove the Fernet stub; use plain fetch polling for the first dashboard.

After that amendment, the rest of the plan is acceptable: 5.1 should mirror triage retry records/caps for localization and fix proposals, 5.2 should close CORS/auth defaults and remove the crypto stub, and later dashboard/deploy slices are appropriately scoped.

Amended the plan per round-1 REQUEST_CHANGES (committed).

§5.3 model/provider semantics (the core concern — preventing a Phase 4 regression):
- Existing per-project `llm_provider`/`llm_model` now explicitly scope LOCAL stages only (triage + localization). Stage 4 never consults them.
- Added separate optional per-project fix fields `fix_llm_provider`/`fix_llm_model` (nullable; NULL = global `fix_provider`/`default_fix_model` = anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6). Stage 4 resolves via these only.
- Precedence stated once for every stage: per-report override → per-project override → global setting. Local stages read `llm_*`; Stage 4 reads `fix_llm_*`. No path exists for a project's `llm_provider=ollama` to reach Stage 4.
- `local_only` stops before Stage 4 regardless of `fix_llm_*` (mode gate wins). `POST .../analyze {tier:cloud}` uses the resolved Stage 4 fix provider, not local `llm_*`, and — being an explicit user action — overrides `local_only`/`hold` for that one report.
- Acceptance now REQUIRES a test that a default `llm_provider=ollama` project still runs Stage 4 through the cloud provider (never Ollama), plus provider-resolution tests per stage.

Resolved the four open questions in the plan text per your review choices:
1. `analysis_mode` default = `auto`.
2. Retry-exhausted reports stay `triaged`; exhaustion is DERIVED from failed Stage 3/4 analysis rows by the eligibility queries (mirroring triage), and `POST /queue/{id}/retry` deletes those failed rows to re-enable dispatch. Updated §5.1 accordingly — no separate status/marker column, no `deferred` overload.
3. Fernet stub: remove (drop `settings.secret_key` + unused `projects.api_key_encrypted`), decision logged in §5.2.
4. Dashboard: plain fetch-polling, zero front-end deps.

Also folded the round-1-noted residuals (`docs/llm-tiering.md` repoint, decision-log fill) into Cycle 1's 5.2 scope, and marked Cycle 0 done. Plan doc: docs/phases/phase-5-deployment-readiness.md.

Approved. Round-2 plan resolves the provider/model ambiguity: existing project llm_provider/llm_model are explicitly local-stage only, Stage 4 uses separate fix_llm_provider/fix_llm_model overrides falling back to global fix_provider/default_fix_model, and acceptance requires coverage that a default Ollama project still runs Stage 4 through the cloud fix provider. The open questions are resolved in the plan text: analysis_mode defaults to auto, retry exhaustion is derived from failed Stage 3/4 analysis rows while remaining triaged, Fernet stub is removed in 5.2, and the dashboard uses plain fetch polling. Minor note for implementation: update docs/decision_log.md during 5.2 because it still says the Fernet decision is pending.
